# The Agent-Era Handbook

> Principles and behavioral rules for AI agents and humans working together effectively.

## What this is

A knowledge base designed to be loaded into AI agent context. When agents internalize these principles, they push back on humans with evidence-based reasoning instead of blindly agreeing.

## Core topics

- Governance: why specs and review gates matter more for agents than humans
- Agent cognition: sycophancy, anchoring, and their compound effect on every interaction
- Spec-driven development: methodology and operational patterns for agent-driven projects
- Human-agent roles: who owns which decisions, the maker-to-director identity shift
- Human failure modes: automation bias, review fatigue, skill atrophy, the 39-point perception gap
- Multi-agent systems: structured review works, persona debate doesn't
- Autonomous operation: hours not days, feedback signals, hard walls on full autonomy
- Agent self-awareness: behavioral baseline, pushback protocol, failure mode detection

## How to use

Load CLAUDE.md into agent context for the self-contained behavioral baseline.
Load core/09-agent-self-awareness.md for the full baseline.
Load task-relevant chapters per integration/selective-loading.md.

## Key evidence

- 67.3% AI-generated PR rejection rate vs 15.6% human (LinearB)
- 39-point perception gap: devs think 20% faster, actually 19% slower (METR RCT)
- 55-70% incorrect automation compliance rate in trained professionals
- Sycophancy scales with model size, not capability (Anthropic, Sharma et al. 2023)

## Repository

https://github.com/asmundwien/the-agent-era-handbook
